description_long: This object is a large, rectangular knife with a distinctive stepped blade design, featuring a series of parallel grooves running along its length. The blade appears to be integrated into a solid, block-like body, with the stepped section forming a sharp, triangular pattern at one end. The handle is a simple rectangular extension, seamlessly connected to the main body without any visible ergonomic shaping or grip texture. The top surface is dominated by the dark, grooved blade, while the sides and bottom are smooth and light-colored, likely metallic. The overall construction suggests a modern, industrial aesthetic, possibly inspired by brutalist design. There are no visible decorative elements or a guard, and the object appears robust and utilitarian.
description: A large, blocky knife with a stepped, grooved blade and a simple rectangular handle, featuring a modern, industrial look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the knife, with the stepped blade and rectangular handle clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the triangular, stepped blade pattern and the width of the handle extension.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's grooves and the stepped geometry from a mirrored perspective.
description_view_3: View from the handle end, showing the grooved blade surface and the open section beneath the handle.
attribute_handle_design: rectangular, smooth, integrated, no visible grip texture
